items.title: Ahmad Maslan says government finalising Engineers Scale of Fees review to set fairer pay benchmark
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The government is finalising a review of the Engineers Scale of Fees, which was gazetted in 1998, to ensure that professional engineering service fees are more in line with current economic realities and the operating costs of engineering consultancy firms. He said the amendments would replace the existing maximum fee structure with a new minimum fee benchmark to ensure that engineers receive professional remuneration comparable to that of other professionals in the construction industry. Ahmad said that once gazetted, the revised fee scale will be mandatory for all professional engineers and engineering consultancy practices (ECPs) operating in both the public and private sectors.

items.title: Fire tears through Subang Jaya apartment, four residents rescued after being trapped by thick smoke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Four residents were rescued after a fire gutted a first-floor apartment at Subang Perdana Goodyear Court 7 in USJ today, trapping occupants in an upper-floor unit due to thick smoke. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said firefighters received an emergency call at 10.02am and arrived at the apartment block in Jalan USJ 14/1 about 10 minutes later, reported Harian Metro . The fire destroyed an estimated 80 per cent of the first-floor apartment.

items.title: New MOH rule compels medicine suppliers to report shortages from July 1, ensuring stability despite West Asia risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Effective July 1, the Health Ministry (MOH) will enforce mandatory reporting by Product Registration Holders (PRHs) on any disruptions or discontinuation of medicine supply. The ministry said the mechanism is part of the proactive measures being taken to ensure the stability and control of the country’s imported medicine supply chain, despite risks arising from conflicts in West Asia. On medicine security in Sabah’s public healthcare services, the ministry said supply levels in the state remain stable and under control, despite unique logistical challenges due to geographical factors.

items.title: Dungun man accused of hammer attack on grandmother ordered to undergo psychiatric evaluation
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 29 — The Sessions Court today ordered a 25-year-old man charged with causing grievous hurt to his grandmother with a hammer to undergo a mental health assessment. Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli issued the order against Muhammad Nor Iman Abdullah following an application by the prosecution, which requested that the accused undergo a psychiatric evaluation before any plea or statement is recorded. The court ordered him to be sent to Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta, Perak, to undergo a mental assessment.

items.title: Azalina: Law amended to curb transnational child sexual abuse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Amendments to the Sexual Offences against Children Act are aimed at strengthening legal protection against child sexual exploitation, particularly offences committed across borders. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the move is in line with efforts to enhance protection for children against cross-border sexual crimes, while ensuring the Act remains relevant amid the evolving landscape of sexual offences. She said the legislation was necessary to keep pace with the evolving nature of child sexual crimes, which have become increasingly sophisticated due to advances in digital technology, social media, messaging applications and online platforms.

items.title: Azalina tables sweeping amendment to Act 792 expanding child sex crime law and closing loopholes overseas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The proposed amendment to the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 (Act 792) seeks to ensure that Malaysia does not face jurisdictional constraints in taking action against perpetrators of child sexual crimes, even if the offences are committed abroad. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the move is in line with efforts to strengthen protection for children against cross-border sexual crimes, while ensuring the act remains relevant amid the evolving nature of sexual offences. “At the same time, protection is also extended to children who are Malaysian citizens, permanent residents, or ordinarily reside in Malaysia,” she said.

items.title: Iran, Oman hold first Hormuz talks since US war deal
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran said Monday it had held its first meeting with Oman on managing the Strait of Hormuz since it signed a preliminary deal to end the Middle East war with the United States. Iran and Oman border the strait, through which a fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas passed prior to the conflict. The announcement came hours after a US official told AFP that technical talks with Iran would continue.

items.title: Johor Election: BN unfazed by party-hopping allegations in Endau
items.summary: MERSING: Allegations of party hopping played up by opponents have not affected grassroots support for Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate for the Endau seat, Alwiyah Talib. Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said this was based on the positive reception from the grassroots towards Alwiyah after her return to BN under the "Rumah Bangsa Johor" initiative. Meanwhile, Onn Hafiz assured full support for Alwiyah to ensure BN retains the Endau state seat in the Johor election.

items.title: Two Malaysians injured after IED blast near SUV in Thailand’s Tak Bai, one seriously hurt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Two Malaysians were injured after an improvised explosive device (IED) planted beneath a roadside culvert exploded near their vehicle in Thailand’s Tak Bai district this afternoon, with one of the victims suffering serious injuries. According to Berita Harian, Abdullah suffered shrapnel wounds to his forehead, serious injuries to his left leg and head injuries believed to have been caused by shattered glass, while Muhammad Yusri sustained minor injuries. “The victims’ vehicle was also damaged in the bomb blast.

items.title: Johor polls: PH candidate claims campaign sabotage in Tanjung Surat
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Pakatan Harapan (PH) Tanjung Surat candidate Faizul Abdul Ghani has lodged a police report and submitted a complaint to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) over an alleged fake Facebook account and claims of campaign interference. He said a Facebook page using the name "Harapan Pengerang" is not an official account linked to him, his party machinery or PH at the constituency level. He said the account had allegedly published content that created confusion among voters, including posts relating to an incident involving the burning of a PH flag in the constituency.

items.title: Johor polls: Young voters seek fresh ideas, others value experience
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: As Johor heads to the polls on July 11, will first-time voters back fresh political faces or place their trust in experienced incumbents? Despite living under the same roof, three members of the family — representing two generations of voters — hold contrasting views on the qualities that matter most when choosing the state's next elected representatives. Tourism industry player Sheikh Ahmad Fauzi Sheikh Abu Bakar, 54, believes the time has come for younger leaders to step forward.

items.title: Three young Malaysian scientists head to Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting, joining 600 peers worldwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Three young scientists have been selected to represent the country at the 75th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting, which is being held in Lindau, Germany, from June 28 to July 3. The Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (Mosti), in a statement today, announced that the selected representatives are Department of Pharmacology lecturer at the Faculty of Medicine, Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM), Dr Nur Liyana Mohammed Yusof, 34; postdoctoral research fellow at the Department of Physics, Universiti Malaya (UM), Dr Pershaanaa K. Manogran, 29; and postdoctoral fellow at UM Institute of Ocean and Earth Sciences, Dr Ruth Naomi Manuel, 31. According to Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk Chang Lih Kang, Malaysia’s participation in global scientific platforms such as the Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting reflects the government’s commitment to nurturing future-ready talent in line with the aspirations of the 13th Malaysia Plan (2026-2030).

items.title: Court throws out RM9.13mil suit over historic Terengganu waqf land
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 16-year fight to reclaim the historic Kampung Ladang Tok Pelam land in Terengganu ended when the High Court threw out a suit by a descendants' group seeking the return of the property or RM9.13 million in compensation. The court also ruled that the plaintiff had filed the wrong type of action, brought its claim too late, and raised issues that fell within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Syariah Court. The plaintiff had claimed the land was wakaf zuriat (family waqf) belonging to the descendants of Tok Pelam, one of Terengganu's most revered Islamic scholars, and sought either the return of the land or RM9,133,430.27 paid by the state to the Maidam after the land was compulsorily acquired in 2010.

items.title: Trump's Iran deal falls short for some voters, could cost Republicans the midterm
items.summary: PRESIDENT Donald Trump's interim agreement to end the war with Iran has dragged down his approval rating and garnered criticism across the political spectrum — even from his own supporters. Many of the Trump voters feared his unpopular concessions to Iran would make it harder for Republicans to retain control of Congress in November's midterm elections, although those most critical of the deal had already begun to lose faith in the president before the war. Juan Rivera, 26, said Trump "criticised his predecessors about negotiating with terrorists, and he's basically done the same exact thing."

items.title: State election unlikely to derail Johor home sales
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Johor's state election on July 11 is unlikely to have a lasting impact on the state's property market, with any temporary slowdown in home sales expected to be offset in the months that follow. He noted that during 2022, when Johor held a state election in March and Malaysia held a general election in November, residential transactions in the state still increased by more than 25 per cent. Johor also outperformed during the 2013 general election year, recording about 16 per cent growth in residential transactions despite an overall decline in housing transactions nationwide.

items.title: Malaysia Airlines raises ticket prices by 20 to 30pct amid soaring fuel costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) is strengthening its fuel hedging strategy and expanding its fleet as part of efforts to mitigate rising operational costs driven by volatile global fuel prices. President and group chief executive officer Captain Nasaruddin A. Bakar said fuel currently accounts for about 30 per cent of Malaysia Airlines' operating costs, but this had risen to around 50 per cent following geopolitical tensions since late February. "First and foremost, fuel costs for Malaysia Airlines are about 30 per cent.

items.title: Trainocate marks 30 years with AI skills programme for underserved youth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Trainocate Malaysia marks its 30th anniversary by launching an initiative to equip underserved youths with practical artificial intelligence (AI) skills to improve their employability. The IT and professional development training provider said the TrAInocate Career Jumpstart 30 programme trained 116 youths from welfare homes, community organisations and community colleges across three cohorts throughout June. "TrAInocate Career Jumpstart 30 goes beyond teaching technology.

items.title: Two Malaysians identified as injured in Thailand roadside bomb blast
items.summary: Narathiwat provincial police commander Major General Prayong Kotsakha said Abdullah, who is from Kelantan, suffered shrapnel wounds to his forehead, a serious injury to his left leg and head injuries. Yusri, from Sabah, sustained minor injuries. He said Yusri suffered only minor injuries and had no visible wounds.

items.title: Australia to give regulator more power to pursue Big Tech over under-16 ban
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australia will introduce new laws in parliament on Monday to strengthen its under-16 social media ban and give its internet regulator more power to pursue tech giants in court for non-compliance. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said there were still too many children on social media and tech firms were not doing enough to comply with the law. "Today, we'll introduce legislation this afternoon that goes further to ensure social media companies are doing everything within their power to stop children under-16 being on their platforms."

items.title: 'No more 3-hour jams': Shah Alam LRT3 line starts operations today
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Shah Alam LRT3 line officially commenced operations today, linking commuters across Klang, Shah Alam, and Petaling Jaya. Nurshuhada Yeob, 32, who works in Petaling Jaya, said the new rail link offers a much-needed alternative to daily traffic congestion. With this LRT line, Johan Setia will definitely grow further," she said.

items.title: Japan's oyster ice cream goes viral
items.summary: IWATE: A soft-serve ice cream topped with fresh oysters from Yamada Bay in Japan's Iwate Prefecture has gone viral after drawing crowds this summer, Oddity Central reported. The roadside station Michino-eki Yamada has reportedly been flooded with visitors eager to try the unusual dessert. The dish features creamy, milk-based soft-serve ice cream paired with fresh oysters sourced locally from Yamada Bay.

items.title: SME optimism holds for 2026 as costs continue to rise – UOB
items.summary: According to UOB, persistent cost pressures are reshaping business priorities this year, with energy management emerging as an increasingly important focus area for companies seeking to improve operational efficiency and manage expenses. Among the key priorities identified were reducing energy consumption (48 per cent), lowering energy costs (44 per cent) and ensuring a stable and reliable energy supply (40 per cent). UOB Malaysia chief executive officer Ng Wei Wei said businesses continue to face cost-related challenges, including higher energy and fuel expenses, making energy management a practical priority for many companies.

items.title: Puad claims secret Umno‑PAS talks bred overconfidence, now BN faces precarious situation
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 29 — Former Johor assembly speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi claimed that the secret plot between Umno and PAS to form a political partnership for the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections had led to the former being conceited. Puad, who was a former Umno supreme council member, revealed that the initial agreement required PAS to sit out the Johor state election, paving the way for a Barisan Nasional (BN) victory in all 56 constituencies. “Among the Umno leaders, Datuk Megat (political secretary to the deputy prime minister Datuk Wira Megat Zulkarnain Omardin) believed that if the negotiations were successful Umno can win big in Johor.

items.title: Malaysia's fund management industry poised to top US$300bil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's fund management industry is expected to surpass US$300 billion in assets under management (AUM) in the second half of 2026 and into 2027. Fitch said total industry AUM rose 9.5 per cent year-on-year to RM1.1 trillion (US$283 billion) at the end of the first quarter of 2026, equivalent to about 60 per cent of Malaysia's GDP. Islamic funds accounted for 23.9 per cent of industry AUM, with assets growing 19.5 per cent year-on-year, outpacing the 6.8 per cent growth recorded by conventional funds.

items.title: 32,800 completed homes worth RM16.4bil remain unsold nationwide
items.summary: Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu said that of the total, 15,400 units, or 46.9 per cent, were homes priced at RM300,000 and below. "The situation shows that the issue of unsold homes is not confined to affordable housing, but is also driven by a mismatch between supply and demand across various segments of the housing market," she said during the question-and-answer session in the Dewan Rakyat today. "Through the new National Housing Policy, which is being finalised, the government is placing emphasis on providing housing that is more responsive to the needs of the people, strengthening the housing financing ecosystem, enhancing the integrated housing database, and reducing the mismatch between housing supply and market demand," she said.

items.title: Johor polls: Umno man wishes PN brother well in Kahang fight
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Former Tebrau Umno division chief Datuk Maulizan Bujang wishes his younger brother, Mazlan, who is contesting under Perikatan Nasional (PN), success in the Johor election despite their differing political affiliations in the Kahang seat. Maulizan, who is backing the Barisan Nasional candidate for Puteri Wangsa, said he has never interfered with his brother's political stance, even as the latter contests under a different party. "Even though we belong to different political parties, we will always remain brothers," he said.

items.title: TPK Park enters record books as largest home and living retail cluster
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: TPK Park, formerly known as Taman Perindustrian Kinrara, has been recognised by the Malaysia Book of Records as the country's largest home and living retail cluster within an industrial park. The recognition marks the culmination of a three-decade transformation that has seen the industrial park evolve into a retail destination housing complementary home and living businesses under one location. TPK Park managing director Datuk Wong Shung Yen said the recognition reflected a long-term effort to create a business ecosystem that benefits both tenants and consumers.

items.title: Government to ensure lower global oil prices translate into cheaper goods: Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will continue monitoring the decline in global oil prices and ensure the lower costs are passed on to consumers through cheaper goods, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said. The minister said that although Malaysia had successfully contained sharp price increases during the energy crisis through targeted subsidy programmes such as Budi Madani and the Subsidised Diesel Control System, the government's focus has now shifted to ensuring consumers benefit from the downward trend in energy prices. Akmal Nasrullah said, besides monitoring prices, the government was also paying close attention to the financial position of micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) to ensure they maintained a healthy cash flow amid economic uncertainty.

items.title: Johor election: 'Let voters decide my capability,' says Orang Asli candidate
items.summary: MERSING: As the sole Parti Orang Asli Malaysia candidate contesting in the Johor election, Jati Awang is aware that the odds are stacked against him. The 52-year-old, however, is unfazed and said that his candidacy is about giving Orang Asli and ordinary Malaysians a new voice rather than worrying about the profile of his rivals. "This is the first time an Orang Asli-based political party is contesting an election in our country.

items.title: Home Ministry explores AI to predict maritime threats, to expand use of UAVs
items.summary: Deputy Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said the ministry has also agreed to expand the use of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), including drones, with phased deployment planned across national waters, including Langkawi. He said UAVs would act as a force multiplier to existing maritime and patrol assets by enabling real-time aerial surveillance, particularly over vast, high-risk areas with limited monitoring coverage. Shamsul Anuar also said that the ministry is reviewing a proposal to establish an Integrated Forward Operating Base in Teluk Ewa, Langkawi, which would house the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA), Marine Police Force and General Operations Force.

items.title: Syed Saddiq to learn fate in final appeal tomorrow
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman will know whether he remains a free man or faces jail time over corruption-related charges when the Federal Court delivers its decision tomorrow. The prosecution, led by deputy public prosecutor Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Ladin, is seeking to reinstate the High Court ruling which sentenced the former minister to seven years behind bars, two strokes of the rotan and a RM10 million fine. For the prosecution, the appeal represents a final push to restore the High Court's findings.

items.title: 'One-time opportunity': South Korea bets big on AI boom
items.summary: Seoul announced Monday massive investments, including for new semiconductor factories and AI data centres, led by South Korean chipmakers. Three companies dominate the global market for producing advanced memory chips that help power AI systems: US giant Micron, and South Korea's Samsung Electronics and SK hynix. "AI has not only provided big demand, it has also created shortages, and that has driven price escalation," Jim Handy, semiconductor expert at Objective Analysis, told AFP.

items.title: Deputy transport minister: Shah Alam LRT line cost cuts won't cause overcrowding
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The 2018 decision to cut costs for the Shah Alam LRT Line, previously known as LRT3, will not result in overcrowded coaches during peak hours, said the Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah. "According to the planning, the second phase (of the Shah Alam Line) involves adding five more stations and seven more train sets. Meanwhile, Hasbi said the project has the capacity to accommodate an estimated 223,560 passengers a day, with an estimated 6,210 passengers per hour per direction.

items.title: LBS Foundation rolls out 'Project AKAR' to support schools, students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: LBS Bina Group Bhd has launched Project AKAR, a long-term school adoption programme under its philanthropic arm, LBS Foundation, beginning with Sekolah Kebangsaan Sungei Way in Petaling Jaya. The launch was officiated by Selangor state executive councillor for Islamic affairs and innovation culture Dr Mohammad Fahmi Ngah, together with LBS Foundation committee members led by Tan Sri Dr Lim Hock San, as well as school administrators, teachers and students. LBS Foundation chairman Tan Sri Dr Lim Hock San said Project AKAR reflects the foundation's belief that lasting social change begins with education.

items.title: M'sian injured after X50 hit by bomb blast in Thailand
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A Malaysian man was injured after his vehicle was caught in a bomb blast in Kampung Sapom, Tak Bai, Thailand, this afternoon. The 38-year-old victim suffered injuries to his body, while his vehicle was also damaged in the explosion. "His vehicle was also damaged in the explosion," he said.

items.title: Global supply crisis may push fertiliser costs up 20pct
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Fertiliser costs are projected to rise by 15 to 20 per cent amid ongoing global supply disruptions, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said. He said 63 per cent of Malaysia's fertiliser supply is imported, leaving the sector vulnerable to global price shocks. Akmal said rising logistics and agricultural input costs were putting pressure on food prices.

items.title: Court of Appeal rules survivor cannot alter mutual will unilaterally
items.summary: In a 2-1 majority decision, the three-member bench chaired by Datuk Mohamed Zaini Mazlan held that the doctrine of mutual wills became binding upon the death of the first testator. After Dennis died in 2019, Charanjeet Kaur Sukhbir Singh, who had been appointed executor under the 2007 wills, challenged the validity of the 2014 will, arguing that the earlier wills were mutual wills and therefore irrevocable after Jaspal's death. However, the appellate court reversed the decision after finding sufficient evidence that Dennis and Jaspal intended the 2007 wills to operate as mutual wills.

items.title: Johor election: Layang-Layang BN candidate stresses unity as core campaign pillar
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM: Barisan Nasional candidate for Layang-Layang Chua Jian Boon is placing strong emphasis on unity among Johor's multiracial communities, saying development must go hand in hand with social cohesion. Chua said economic progress would lose its meaning if harmony among the state's main communities is not preserved. "There is no point in chasing development and progress in Johor if we fail to maintain social cohesion, especially unity among the three main races in the state," he told the New Straits Times.

items.title: Dzulkefly tells Dewan Rakyat MOH reviewing cerebral palsy registry plan to widen rehab access
items.summary: Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the proposal is aimed at expanding patients’ access to rehabilitation facilities, including through collaboration with the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) rehabilitation centres. “MOH welcomes the proposal to establish a National Cerebral Palsy Registry and is also reviewing efforts to expand patients’ access to rehabilitation facilities, including through collaboration with Perkeso rehabilitation centres,” he said when replying to a question from Dr Kelvin Yii Lee Wuen (PH-Bandar Kuching). Dzulkefly said MOH assists CP patients in obtaining rehabilitation equipment through the Medical Assistance Fund, the Malaysian Medical Welfare Fund and the National Cancer Council, based on clinical assessment and patients’ eligibility.

items.title: Cabinet greenlights MH370 deep-sea search extension with Ocean Infinity for another year, search to resume July 1, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The government has agreed to extend its agreement with UK-based Ocean Infinity for another 12 months to continue deep-sea efforts to locate Malaysia Airlines flight MH370 in the southern Indian Ocean. Transport Minister Loke Siew Fook said the Cabinet approved the extension on June 26, with the new contract period running from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2027. Loke said the decision also takes into account Ocean Infinity’s new commercial commitments, which require its main operational assets to be temporarily redeployed elsewhere.

items.title: Ex‑Temerloh police chief ordered to enter defence on 46 bribery charges totalling RM69,600
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 29 — The Sessions Court here today ordered former Temerloh district police chief Mohd Azhar Mohd Yusoff to enter his defence on 46 counts of accepting bribes totalling RM69,600 between 2019 and 2023. “The court finds and rules that the prosecution has successfully established a prima facie case on all 46 principal charges faced by the accused under Section 165 of the Penal Code. Accordingly, the court ordered Mohd Azhar to enter his defence on all the charges.

items.title: PM Anwar to roll out redesigned Malaysian passport tomorrow, holograms and UV ink among 94 features
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to launch the new version of the Malaysian International Passport, equipped with 94 security features, at the Parliament lobby Tuesday. The new passport features a significant enhancement over the current version, which has only 49 security features, making it more secure and difficult to forge. Among the upgraded features are holographic elements, ultraviolet (UV) printing, hidden visual elements and several specialised forensic security features.

items.title: Anwar unveils Malaysia Digital 2030: Targets 30pc GDP, 500,000 jobs, 95pc services online by 2030
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today launched Malaysia Digital 2030 (MD2030), a national action plan for 2026-2030 that marks a major shift in the country’s digital strategy from being a technology consumer to a producer of home-grown innovation. The launch was attended by Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar. In a statement, the Digital Ministry said the action plan sets ambitious national targets by 2030, including raising the digital economy’s contribution to gross domestic product (GDP) to 30 per cent, creating 500,000 high-value digital jobs, generating RM4.5 billion in public sector savings through digitalisation, and delivering 95 per cent of government services fully online from end to end.

items.title: Maszlee Malik dismisses MB speculation, says Johor Sultan holds sole prerogative under state constitution
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 29 — Pakatan Harapan will leave the appointment of the Johor Menteri Besar entirely to the wisdom and prerogative of the Sultan of Johor should the coalition succeed in forming the state government in the state election. PH’s candidate for the Puteri Wangsa state seat, Dr Maszlee Malik, said the coalition has not yet discussed the post as its main focus is to provide the best possible service to the people through teamwork. He said this when commenting on speculation circulating on social media linking him as one of the leading candidates for the post of Johor Menteri Besar from the PH bloc should the coalition win the 16th Johor state polls.

items.title: World Cup fever fuels illegal betting surge as police net 331 in nationwide Ops Soga XI crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Police have intensified nationwide enforcement against illegal sports betting ahead of the Fifa World Cup 2026, with more than 300 suspects arrested under an ongoing coordinated operation targeting online gambling activities. Police also seized RM145,005.09 in cash and identified betting transactions worth RM2.63 million linked to the illegal activities. Authorities said 185 websites, applications, platforms and social media accounts were detected promoting or facilitating illegal football betting during the operation.

items.title: Putrajaya says shielding SMEs from war fallout top priority, RM700m in financial help already given up to June
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Bank Negara Malaysia had approved over RM700 million in relief financing up to June, Economy Minister Akmal Nasir Nasrullah told Parliament this morning, as he pledged to make protection of small and medium businesses from the global supply crisis a top priority. The Johor Bahru MP said the fund from BNM’s SME Stabilisation Relief Facility had been extended to some 1,000 SMEs up to June 18. “Affected SMEs received cash flow support through the BNM SME Stabilisation Relief Facility (SME SRF), totalling RM5 billion...more than RM4 billion remains available, indicating ample room for subsequent applications,” Akmal said during a special communication session in Parliament’s Lower House this morning.

items.title: Kajang cops hunt man suspected of impersonating civil servant to dupe public
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Police are tracking down a man suspected of impersonating a civil servant by wearing a security force uniform and using a background resembling a government office to deceive members of the public. Kajang police chief ACP Naazron Abdul Yusof said a report regarding the incident was received at 6.58pm yesterday. Naazron also urged the public to report any suspected criminal activity through the MERS 999 emergency line or the Kajang District Police Control Centre at 03-8911 4243.

items.title: Housing mismatch persists as 32,800 unsold homes worth RM16.3b recorded in Q1 2026, Dewan Rakyat told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Malaysia is facing a continued mismatch in the housing market, with tens of thousands of completed homes remaining unsold even as affordability challenges persist, the Ministry of Housing and Local Government told Dewan Rakyat today. “This shows that unsold housing is not limited to affordable housing alone, but is also influenced by a broader mismatch between supply and demand across different market segments,” she said. “Generally, D1 to D4 represent lower income households, D5 to D8 middle income, and D9 to D10 high income households,” she said.

items.title: Malaysia’s risk of mass job losses from global energy crisis remains low, says economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Malaysia’s exposure to the risk of mass scale job losses stemming from the global energy supply crunch remains low, Economy Minister Akmal Nasir Nasrullah told Parliament today, even as the crisis is already putting some pressure on local companies. Domestically, Akmal said the supply chokehold is putting pressure on various sectors, particularly logistics, aviation, shipping, agriculture, manufacturing, construction, and trade. When costs rise sharply, their cash flow is more rapidly affected, Akmal said.

items.title: Firefighter pleads not guilty to negligent driving over crash that injured child in Bachok
items.summary: BACHOK, June 29 — A firefighter pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of negligent driving that allegedly resulted in serious injuries to a four-year-old girl early this year. Mohammad Shuhaili Shuib, 46, entered the plea before Magistrate Fakhrul Razzi Abd Hamid. He was charged with negligently driving a Toyota Innova multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) and hitting the girl at the parking area of Sri Chalet Nipah, causing serious injuries to the child, at about 10.45am on January 3 this year.

items.title: LRT3 Shah Alam debuts smoothly, no women-only coach planned yet, says Prasarana
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 29 — The first day of operations for the LRT3 Shah Alam Line got off to a smooth start today, with Prasarana Malaysia Bhd saying only minor teething issues emerged as it began monitoring passenger behaviour to fine-tune the new service. Outgoing Prasarana president and group chief executive officer Amir Hamdan said train operations, station facilities and passenger movement remained under control throughout the morning, with trains operating at peak-hour headways of around eight minutes. Amir said the first day of operations also gave Prasarana an opportunity to observe passenger flow, particularly at interchange stations where commuters transfer between the Shah Alam Line and existing rail services.

items.title: Malaysia eyes AI, drones to strengthen maritime security, says deputy home minister
items.summary: He said the overall wellbeing of personnel and the condition of operational assets remain strong, supported by ongoing upgrades in capability and coordinated enforcement operations to maintain maritime security. “This is achieved through enhanced operational capabilities supported by sufficient assets and the implementation of enforcement operations to ensure maritime security is consistently maintained. Shamsul Anuar said all enforcement units remain on standby and ready for immediate deployment when required.

items.title: Kg Betangga Highland villagers in Sabah allege land encroachment, urge MACC, police and Native Court probe
items.summary: SIPITANG, June 29 — Villagers from Kg Betangga Highland have called on the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), the police and the Native Court to investigate the alleged encroachment of village land by a company. Village Chairman Pius Ganang said he lodged a report at the Sipitang District Police Headquarters last Saturday, urging the authorities to conduct a thorough investigation into the matter. He claimed Kg Betangga Highland is part of Kg Mendulong, one of the former Sabah Forest Industries (SFI) concession areas that had been earmarked for gazettement as a village.

items.title: Johor polls: Police open three probes as campaigning gets underway
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 29 — Johor police have received seven reports related to the Johor state election since nomination day last Saturday until 11.59pm last night. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said that of the total, three investigation papers have been opened involving various offences. To date, Ab Rahaman said police have received 420 permit applications for political campaign activities.

items.title: LRT3 Phase Two set to add five stations, seven trains under long-term expansion plan, deputy minister says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Phase Two of the Shah Alam Light Rail Transit Line 3 (LRT3) project remains part of the government’s long term development plan and will include five additional stations and seven more train sets, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said the existing infrastructure had also been designed to support future expansion should passenger demand increase. Responding to the concerns, Hasbi acknowledged that the 2018 review had led to substantial revisions to the project, but stressed that current capacity was sufficient based on projected passenger demand.

items.title: Selangor religious council vows action after family ordeal over delayed burial at Ukay Perdana Muslim Cemetery
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 29 — The Selangor Islamic Religious Council (Mais) has directed that an urgent meeting be held between the deceased’s next of kin, the management of Masjid Nurul Hidayah, Kampung Pandan Dalam, the Salatulrahim Welfare Organisation (BKS), and the Selangor Islamic Religious Department (JAIS) to resolve the issue surrounding allegations of delays in the burial of a deceased person at the Ukay Perdana Muslim Cemetery in Hulu Kelang. Mais chairman Datuk Salehuddin Saidin said the meeting is intended to achieve the best possible resolution for all parties involved while ensuring that a similar incident does not occur in the future. He said that Mais had also been informed that the mosque management, the deceased’s family, and the BKS had each lodged police reports regarding the incident.

items.title: Woman’s body found in suitcase after smell leads landlord to Kajang flat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — A landlord said he was alerted to a foul smell and extremely dirty conditions inside a rented unit in Taman Impian Indah here, which eventually led to the discovery of a suitcase containing the body of a woman believed to be a Nepalese tenant. He said he had been unable to contact the tenant on several occasions, prompting him to check the property himself, Harian Metro reported today. I then found the suitcase,” he said when met on Saturday.

items.title: Targeted diesel subsidy plan sparks concern in Sabah over rural access
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 29 — As Putrajaya prepares to roll out its targeted diesel subsidy scheme in just a few days, concerns are mounting that a policy designed to curb fuel leakages may inadvertently leave behind many ordinary Sabahans who depend on diesel-powered vehicles for their daily lives. Unlike much of Peninsular Malaysia, diesel is more than just commercial fuel in Sabah. “KGKS members believe that limiting the diesel subsidy quota will affect teachers who commute daily to work, particularly in rural areas.

items.title: Sabah to face rolling power cuts after Petronas gas platform disruption hits grid
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 29 — Thousands of electricity consumers across Sabah will face scheduled power rationing after a disruption at an offshore gas platform reduced electricity generation capacity in the state’s power grid. The reduced gas supply has in turn lowered electricity generation capacity within Sabah’s grid. Sabah Electricity said the scheduled power rationing was necessary to ensure the overall stability of the state’s electricity supply while efforts continue to restore generation capacity.

items.title: Maybank says not under investigation in Indonesia, confirms cooperation with authorities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Malayan Banking Bhd has said it is not under investigation in Indonesia, while confirming that its Indonesian subsidiary is cooperating with authorities over enquiries involving certain corporate customers. The group’s clarification came amid reports of regulatory scrutiny, according to Free Malaysia Today, which said Maybank Indonesia personnel had been asked to provide information as witnesses to assist ongoing investigations. Maybank said its subsidiary is working with the relevant authorities and that neither the group nor its Indonesian unit is the subject of any investigation.
